citations.text: Reused from the same variation-selection-retention framework already cited on OE-CONCEPT-000213 (Mesoudi, A. (2011). Cultural Evolution: How Darwinian Theory Can Explain Human Culture and Synthesize the Social Sciences), applied here to language specifically rather than culture in general.

en.general: The process by which languages change, diversify, and descend from common ancestral languages over time, through the same general transmission-with-variation dynamics that shape other cultural traits.

examples: Reconstructing a language family tree from shared and divergent features across related languages.
